sections.heading: i. Current State
sections.content_instructions:
Describe how the relevant UPI flow works TODAY before this change. Explain the existing mechanism, who the participants are, and how the current process operates. Minimum 2 paragraphs. Business language only — no XML tags or API field names.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment follows a request‑response sequence where the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI obtains authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns AC/IFSC, NPCI then debits the payer (or voucher) via ReqDebit and credits the merchant via ReqCredit, finally a RespTxnConfirmation is sent back to the payer PSP. Amount entry is manual and no system‑driven suggestion is provided.
Limitations / Pain Points: Users must manually type the amount, leading to slower checkout and higher chance of entry errors. PSPs and merchants have no visibility of historical payment patterns to auto‑populate common amounts, which can affect user experience especially for repeat contacts or frequent merchants.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** What is the payer identifier type to be used for smart‑amount suggestions (e.g., VPA, mobile number, Aadhaar)?
A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the payee identifier type that the suggestion engine should map to (e.g., VPA, QR‑code, mobile number)?
A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the maximum amount allowed per transaction for the smart‑suggestion flow?
A: The maximum amount will follow standard UPI limits (typically up to ₹1 lakh per transaction, or higher for specific categories), with no separate cap for the smart-suggestion flow.

Q: **[Required]** What is the aggregate daily transaction limit for payments made using suggested amounts?
A: The aggregate daily limit will remain aligned with standard UPI caps (typically ₹1 lakh/day per user), with suggested-amount payments counted within this overall limit and no separate threshold.

Q: **[Required]** What settlement timeline should apply to transactions initiated via smart‑amount suggestions?
A: Transactions via smart-amount suggestions will follow standard UPI settlement, i.e., T+0 real-time debit/credit with backend interbank settlement handled in the regular clearing cycle.

Q: **[Required]** Which new API messages are required to fetch and confirm smart amount suggestions?
A: No new APIs are strictly required—existing ReqPay can be reused with optional suggestion metadata, though a lightweight FetchSuggestions (pre-call) API can be added for better personalization and caching.

Q: **[Required]** What schema changes are needed in the existing request/response structures to carry suggestion data?
A: Add an optional suggestionContext/suggestedAmount field (with metadata like source, confidence, and lastTxnRef) in the request, and echo it in the response for traceability—kept non-mandatory to preserve backward compatibility.
sections.prompt_instruction: Write 2 paragraphs: (1) the existing process and participants, (2) how the current system handles the relevant scenario.

sections.heading: ii. Limitations / Challenges
sections.content_instructions:
Explain why the current state is insufficient. Cover security gaps, friction points, scalability concerns, or regulatory gaps. Use bullet points or numbered items for each limitation. Minimum 1 paragraph + 4 bullet points.

sections.heading: i. Product Description
sections.content_instructions:
Provide a high-level description of what this feature/product does and what business outcome it achieves. Name the affected participants and the scope of changes. Business language only. Minimum 2 paragraphs.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State (use in Background section):
  Today a UPI payment follows a request‑response sequence where the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI obtains authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns AC/IFSC, NPCI then debits the payer (or voucher) via ReqDebit and credits the merchant via ReqCredit, finally a RespTxnConfirmation is sent back to the payer PSP. Amount entry is manual and no system‑driven suggestion is provided.
Limitations (use in Background section):
  Users must manually type the amount, leading to slower checkout and higher chance of entry errors. PSPs and merchants have no visibility of historical payment patterns to auto‑populate common amounts, which can affect user experience especially for repeat contacts or frequent merchants.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The system shall capture the Amount field from the user interface.
  FR-02: The system shall enforce that for Amount >15000 a UPI_PIN must be included in the Cred block (per Revised Testcases CZ).
  FR-03: The system shall include InitiationMode=11 and PurposeCode=14 in the ReqPay payload when applicable.
  FR-04: The system shall invoke ReqAuthDetails after ReqPay as defined in e‑RUPI flow.
  FR-05: The system shall send RespTxnConfirmation to the payer PSP after successful debit/credit.
  FR-06: The system shall allow the user to edit a suggested amount before final submission.
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback process applies unchanged.
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement ReqPay, ReqAuthDetails, ReqDebit, ReqCredit as per NPCI specifications; must validate amount thresholds and enforce PIN entry rules
  Issuer Banks: must support real‑time debit of credit/voucher accounts; must respond to ReqDebit/ReqCredit with appropriate status
  NPCI: will route ReqPay to Payee PSP after receiving authentication request; will validate InitiationMode and PurposeCode values

Flow Sequence (business language):
  ["Step 1: Payer PSP \u2014 initiates ReqPay with Amount, VPA, InitiationMode=11, PurposeCode=14", "Step 2: NPCI \u2014 forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP", "Step 3: Payee PSP \u2014 validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetail with AC/IFSC", "Step 4: NPCI \u2014 issues ReqDebit to debit payer/voucher", "Step 5: Payee PSP \u2014 receives credit via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation", "Step 6: Payer PSP \u2014 receives RespTxnConfirmation and presents final status to user"]
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** What is the payer identifier type to be used for smart‑amount suggestions (e.g., VPA, mobile number, Aadhaar)?
A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the payee identifier type that the suggestion engine should map to (e.g., VPA, QR‑code, mobile number)?
A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the maximum amount allowed per transaction for the smart‑suggestion flow?
A: The maximum amount will follow standard UPI limits (typically up to ₹1 lakh per transaction, or higher for specific categories), with no separate cap for the smart-suggestion flow.

Q: **[Required]** What is the aggregate daily transaction limit for payments made using suggested amounts?
A: The aggregate daily limit will remain aligned with standard UPI caps (typically ₹1 lakh/day per user), with suggested-amount payments counted within this overall limit and no separate threshold.

Q: **[Required]** What settlement timeline should apply to transactions initiated via smart‑amount suggestions?
A: Transactions via smart-amount suggestions will follow standard UPI settlement, i.e., T+0 real-time debit/credit with backend interbank settlement handled in the regular clearing cycle.

Q: **[Required]** Which new API messages are required to fetch and confirm smart amount suggestions?
A: No new APIs are strictly required—existing ReqPay can be reused with optional suggestion metadata, though a lightweight FetchSuggestions (pre-call) API can be added for better personalization and caching.

Q: **[Required]** What schema changes are needed in the existing request/response structures to carry suggestion data?
A: Add an optional suggestionContext/suggestedAmount field (with metadata like source, confidence, and lastTxnRef) in the request, and echo it in the response for traceability—kept non-mandatory to preserve backward compatibility.
sections.prompt_instruction: Write 2-3 paragraphs describing: what the feature does, who it affects, and what problem it solves from a business perspective. No API details.

sections.heading: ii. Product Construct: Setting / Enrollment
sections.content_instructions:
If the feature requires explicit enrollment or opt-in: describe UI placement, consent capture, and the step-by-step enrollment journey with a Roles & Responsibilities table [Step, Activity, Responsible]. If the feature does NOT require a separate enrollment or setting step: write exactly two sentences stating that this section is not applicable for this feature and briefly explain why (e.g., 'This feature does not require a separate enrollment step. Users access it directly from [entry point] without prior registration or opt-in.'). NEVER leave this section blank.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with amount, VPA and optional voucher details"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetail",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction status to NPCI after debit/credit"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits funds from payer (or voucher) as per approved request"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqCredit",
    "response": "RespCredit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Credits funds to merchant or payee account"
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with Amount, VPA, InitiationMode=11, PurposeCode=14
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetail with AC/IFSC
  Step 4: NPCI — issues ReqDebit to debit payer/voucher
  Step 5: Payee PSP — receives credit via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation
  Step 6: Payer PSP — receives RespTxnConfirmation and presents final status to user
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction (amount >15000 mandates PIN as per test case notes)
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens the UPI app and selects a contact or merchant; the app displays a list of previously used amounts.
  Step 2: User taps a suggested amount (or edits it); the app populates the Amount field in the ReqPay request.
  Step 3: Payer PSP sends ReqPay (with InitiationMode=11, PurposeCode=14) to NPCI.
  Step 4: NPCI forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP; Payee PSP validates and returns RespAuthDetail.
  Step 5: NPCI issues ReqDebit; if Amount >15000, the app prompts for UPI_PIN before proceeding.
  Step 6: Payee PSP receives credit via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation.
  Step 7: Payer PSP receives RespTxnConfirmation and shows SUCCESS to the user.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):

A: VPA

A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the maximum amount allowed per transaction for the smart‑suggestion flow?
A: The maximum amount will follow standard UPI limits (typically up to ₹1 lakh per transaction, or higher for specific categories), with no separate cap for the smart-suggestion flow.

Q: **[Required]** What is the aggregate daily transaction limit for payments made using suggested amounts?
A: The aggregate daily limit will remain aligned with standard UPI caps (typically ₹1 lakh/day per user), with suggested-amount payments counted within this overall limit and no separate threshold.

Q: **[Required]** What settlement timeline should apply to transactions initiated via smart‑amount suggestions?
A: Transactions via smart-amount suggestions will follow standard UPI settlement, i.e., T+0 real-time debit/credit with backend interbank settlement handled in the regular clearing cycle.

Q: **[Required]** Which new API messages are required to fetch and confirm smart amount suggestions?
A: No new APIs are strictly required—existing ReqPay can be reused with optional suggestion metadata, though a lightweight FetchSuggestions (pre-call) API can be added for better personalization and caching.

Q: **[Required]** What schema changes are needed in the existing request/response structures to carry suggestion data?
A: Add an optional suggestionContext/suggestedAmount field (with metadata like source, confidence, and lastTxnRef) in the request, and echo it in the response for traceability—kept non-mandatory to preserve backward compatibility.

sections.heading: iii. Product Construct: Transaction Flow

Cover: triggering conditions, authentication path, debit/credit sequence, and a Roles & Responsibilities table with columns [Step, Activity, Responsible]. Rows: Pre-Check → Step 1..N → Post Response. Minimum 1 paragraph + R&R table.

sections.heading: Other Salient Points
sections.content_instructions:
List edge cases, constraints, opt-in/opt-out rules, backward-compatibility requirements, and any operational rules not covered in the Product Construct sections. Minimum 6 numbered points. Each point is one self-contained business rule or constraint.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Policy Rules:
  Rule 1: For any transaction where Amount >15000, UPI_PIN must be present in the request (per Revised Testcases CZ).
  Rule 2: For Amount <=15000 with SeqNum=1, UPI_PIN must also be sent (per Revised Testcases CZ).
  Rule 3: InitiationMode=11 and PurposeCode=14 must be used for UMN based debit requests.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** What is the payer identifier type to be used for smart‑amount suggestions (e.g., VPA, mobile number, Aadhaar)?
A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the payee identifier type that the suggestion engine should map to (e.g., VPA, QR‑code, mobile number)?
A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the maximum amount allowed per transaction for the smart‑suggestion flow?
A: The maximum amount will follow standard UPI limits (typically up to ₹1 lakh per transaction, or higher for specific categories), with no separate cap for the smart-suggestion flow.

Q: **[Required]** What is the aggregate daily transaction limit for payments made using suggested amounts?
A: The aggregate daily limit will remain aligned with standard UPI caps (typically ₹1 lakh/day per user), with suggested-amount payments counted within this overall limit and no separate threshold.

Q: **[Required]** What settlement timeline should apply to transactions initiated via smart‑amount suggestions?
A: Transactions via smart-amount suggestions will follow standard UPI settlement, i.e., T+0 real-time debit/credit with backend interbank settlement handled in the regular clearing cycle.

Q: **[Required]** Which new API messages are required to fetch and confirm smart amount suggestions?
A: No new APIs are strictly required—existing ReqPay can be reused with optional suggestion metadata, though a lightweight FetchSuggestions (pre-call) API can be added for better personalization and caching.

Q: **[Required]** What schema changes are needed in the existing request/response structures to carry suggestion data?
A: Add an optional suggestionContext/suggestedAmount field (with metadata like source, confidence, and lastTxnRef) in the request, and echo it in the response for traceability—kept non-mandatory to preserve backward compatibility.

sections.heading: A. NPCI (UPI Platform & CL) — Setting / Schema / Registration Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all NPCI platform, Common Library (CL), and schema changes required for the enrollment/setting flow. Number each requirement. Cover: new API messages, changed schema fields, CL version changes, configuration flags. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens the UPI app and selects a contact or merchant; the app displays a list of previously used amounts.
  Step 2: User taps a suggested amount (or edits it); the app populates the Amount field in the ReqPay request.
  Step 3: Payer PSP sends ReqPay (with InitiationMode=11, PurposeCode=14) to NPCI.
  Step 4: NPCI forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP; Payee PSP validates and returns RespAuthDetail.
  Step 5: NPCI issues ReqDebit; if Amount >15000, the app prompts for UPI_PIN before proceeding.
  Step 6: Payee PSP receives credit via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation.
  Step 7: Payer PSP receives RespTxnConfirmation and shows SUCCESS to the user.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):

sections.heading: B. NPCI (UPI Platform & CL) — Transaction Flow / Processing Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all NPCI routing, processing, and validation changes for the payment transaction flow. Number each requirement. Cover: refCategory handling, credential validation, switch routing changes, response handling. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with amount, VPA and optional voucher details"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetail",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction status to NPCI after debit/credit"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits funds from payer (or voucher) as per approved request"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqCredit",
    "response": "RespCredit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Credits funds to merchant or payee account"
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with Amount, VPA, InitiationMode=11, PurposeCode=14
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetail with AC/IFSC
  Step 4: NPCI — issues ReqDebit to debit payer/voucher
  Step 5: Payee PSP — receives credit via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation
  Step 6: Payer PSP — receives RespTxnConfirmation and presents final status to user
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction (amount >15000 mandates PIN as per test case notes)
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

sections.heading: A. UPI App / PSP — App-side Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all UPI App and PSP changes required on the app side for enrollment, consent capture, and device binding. Number each requirement. Cover: UI changes, CL SDK invocation, local key storage, device check logic. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State (use in Background section):
  Today a UPI payment follows a request‑response sequence where the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI obtains authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns AC/IFSC, NPCI then debits the payer (or voucher) via ReqDebit and credits the merchant via ReqCredit, finally a RespTxnConfirmation is sent back to the payer PSP. Amount entry is manual and no system‑driven suggestion is provided.
Limitations (use in Background section):
  Users must manually type the amount, leading to slower checkout and higher chance of entry errors. PSPs and merchants have no visibility of historical payment patterns to auto‑populate common amounts, which can affect user experience especially for repeat contacts or frequent merchants.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The system shall capture the Amount field from the user interface.
  FR-02: The system shall enforce that for Amount >15000 a UPI_PIN must be included in the Cred block (per Revised Testcases CZ).
  FR-03: The system shall include InitiationMode=11 and PurposeCode=14 in the ReqPay payload when applicable.
  FR-04: The system shall invoke ReqAuthDetails after ReqPay as defined in e‑RUPI flow.
  FR-05: The system shall send RespTxnConfirmation to the payer PSP after successful debit/credit.
  FR-06: The system shall allow the user to edit a suggested amount before final submission.
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback process applies unchanged.
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement ReqPay, ReqAuthDetails, ReqDebit, ReqCredit as per NPCI specifications; must validate amount thresholds and enforce PIN entry rules
  Issuer Banks: must support real‑time debit of credit/voucher accounts; must respond to ReqDebit/ReqCredit with appropriate status
  NPCI: will route ReqPay to Payee PSP after receiving authentication request; will validate InitiationMode and PurposeCode values

Flow Sequence (business language):
  ["Step 1: Payer PSP \u2014 initiates ReqPay with Amount, VPA, InitiationMode=11, PurposeCode=14", "Step 2: NPCI \u2014 forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP", "Step 3: Payee PSP \u2014 validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetail with AC/IFSC", "Step 4: NPCI \u2014 issues ReqDebit to debit payer/voucher", "Step 5: Payee PSP \u2014 receives credit via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation", "Step 6: Payer PSP \u2014 receives RespTxnConfirmation and presents final status to user"]
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):

A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the payee identifier type that the suggestion engine should map to (e.g., VPA, QR‑code, mobile number)?
A: VPA

Q: **[Required]** What is the maximum amount allowed per transaction for the smart‑suggestion flow?
A: The maximum amount will follow standard UPI limits (typically up to ₹1 lakh per transaction, or higher for specific categories), with no separate cap for the smart-suggestion flow.

Q: **[Required]** What is the aggregate daily transaction limit for payments made using suggested amounts?
A: The aggregate daily limit will remain aligned with standard UPI caps (typically ₹1 lakh/day per user), with suggested-amount payments counted within this overall limit and no separate threshold.

Q: **[Required]** What settlement timeline should apply to transactions initiated via smart‑amount suggestions?
A: Transactions via smart-amount suggestions will follow standard UPI settlement, i.e., T+0 real-time debit/credit with backend interbank settlement handled in the regular clearing cycle.

Q: **[Required]** Which new API messages are required to fetch and confirm smart amount suggestions?
A: No new APIs are strictly required—existing ReqPay can be reused with optional suggestion metadata, though a lightweight FetchSuggestions (pre-call) API can be added for better personalization and caching.

Q: **[Required]** What schema changes are needed in the existing request/response structures to carry suggestion data?
A: Add an optional suggestionContext/suggestedAmount field (with metadata like source, confidence, and lastTxnRef) in the request, and echo it in the response for traceability—kept non-mandatory to preserve backward compatibility.
